tests_run.command: sqlite3 DB-copy: corr/Brier on 44 settled weather trades
tests_run.result: corr(edge,win)=-0.243, corr(edge,pnl)=-0.171; model side-Brier 0.319 vs market 0.247; win edge>=0.25:0.45 vs edge<0.25:0.82

verdicts.claim: The bot's edge signal is ANTI-predictive: larger model-vs-market edge_at_entry predicts FEWER wins and more loss (corr(edge,win) ~= -0.24). edge_at_entry is the NET edge that drives both trade selection and Kelly bet size (backend/core/weather_signals.py:277 edge=net_edge; backend/core/scheduler.py:234; sized up via win_prob in backend/core/sizing.py:37-70).
verdicts.strongest_counter_evidence: The negative correlation is NOT statistically significant at n=44, so a true-zero ("no signal, too few trades") cannot be excluded. Bootstrap 95% CI on corr(edge,win) = [-0.462, +0.050] crosses zero; two-sided permutation p=0.114 (Pearson) and p=0.761 for the cleaner median-split win-rate difference. The point estimate also shrinks most when Tokyo (the worst loser, n=10) is dropped: corr(edge,win) -0.243 -> -0.155, corr(edge,pnl) -0.171 -> -0.056 — i.e. Tokyo contributes a disproportionate share of the effect, though it never flips the sign. So the precise figure "-0.24" should NOT be sold as a significant effect; honest phrasing is "no positive selectivity, robustly negative-leaning, underpowered."
verdicts.robustness_checks: Reproduced on tradingbot_copy.db, 44 settled trades w/ edge (24W/20L, winrate 0.545). (1) Point est: Pearson corr(edge,win)=-0.243 (matches claim), Spearman=-0.205, corr(edge,pnl)=-0.171. (2) Leave-one-city-out (9 cities): corr(edge,win) NEGATIVE in ALL 9 cuts, range [-0.155 drop-tokyo, -0.319 drop-miami]; corr(edge,pnl) negative in all 9 [-0.056..-0.256]. Refutation attempt (one-city artifact) FAILS: dropping Tokyo weakens but does not flip; dropping Miami STRENGTHENS it. (3) Active-only (n=27, nyc/chi/den/tok/par/hk): corr(edge,win)=-0.220, corr(edge,pnl)=-0.207 — holds on both active and all-cities. (4) Bootstrap 10k: corr(edge,win) P(negative)=0.952, CI=[-0.462,+0.050]; corr(edge,pnl) P(neg)=0.883, CI=[-0.408,+0.139]. (5) Interpretable cut: edge tertiles give MONOTONE-DECREASING win rate 0.714 -> 0.467 -> 0.467 and mean P&L +25.1 -> +12.0 -> -21.3; median-split high-edge half winrate 0.500 vs low-edge 0.591 (high-minus-low -0.091, wrong sign). (6) Mechanism confirmed: corr(edge, bet size)=+0.114 (bot does stake more on bigger edge), and the model overconfidence gap WIDENS with edge (stated conviction minus realized winrate: +0.223 low-edge vs +0.297 high-edge) — the biggest edges are the biggest mirages, consistent with the audit's overconfidence finding.
verdicts.caveats: n=44 is underpowered; the 95% CI just crosses zero so the magnitude is not significant — the durable, load-bearing finding is the ABSENCE of positive selectivity (bootstrap upper bound on a positive correlation is only ~+0.05), not a precise -0.24. Pearson with a binary outcome (point-biserial) is sensitive to the heavy/skewed edge tail; Spearman (-0.205) and the tertile win-rates agree, mitigating but not eliminating this. City inference is parsed from event_slug. Result/pnl are as-settled in the DB copy (no re-grading). GAP + FIX: the decision variable that scales Kelly bet size up (edge = model_side_prob - market_price) has, historically, zero-to-negative predictive value — the most 'selective' high-edge trades did WORSE (T3 winrate 0.467, mean P&L -21.3) and are where the model is most overconfident (+0.297 gap). Until the model is recalibrated, raw edge magnitude is not a protective gate and Kelly-on-edge is mis-sizing toward the mirages. Fixes: (a) flatten/cap sizing vs edge or invert the convexity (do not bet bigger on bigger disagreement with the market) pending recalibration; (b) tighten the market-gap guardrail and apply a shrinkage/probability-calibration layer (e.g. blend model prob toward market price) so stated conviction matches the realized 0.545 winrate; (c) judge on Brier per the run plan, not P&L, and require demonstrated POSITIVE edge-vs-win correlation on a larger sample before trusting edge to size.

verdicts.claim: The model is grossly overconfident (mean stated side-prob ~0.80 vs realized win ~0.545, +0.25 gap) while the market is calibrated (~0.53 vs 0.545).
verdicts.strongest_counter_evidence: Two genuine counter-points, neither of which overturns the claim. (1) The calib_records `center` is analysis-grade / short-lead (look-ahead-flattered) per the lead auditor, so it is a BETTER center than the live forecast. But this makes the finding CONSERVATIVE, not invalid: pricing this flattered center at the live 5.0F width STILL yields NO-bet side-prob 0.844 vs realized 0.545 (gap +0.299, cluster-bootstrap-by-event 95% CI [+0.265,+0.335]); the live bot with a worse center is necessarily >= this overconfident. (2) The model is NOT uniformly broken — at a snooped best-fit sigma (~1.5F, the calibration_backfill width) it reaches near-parity Brier, and its high-confidence open-tail buckets (0.9-1.0 YES bin: pred 0.983, realized 1.000) are well-calibrated. The miscalibration is concentrated in the 0.2-0.5 YES region (mid-confidence buckets near its forecast center: predicts 24.5/34.2/43.6%, realizes 1.9/3.1/29.2%). So "grossly overconfident" is precisely true for the traded region and the cause is identifiable (mass too concentrated around a slightly-off center at the deployed 5.0F width) — which is why it is fixable, not evidence against the claim.
verdicts.robustness_checks: Reproduced live-DB metric exactly (n=44: model side-prob 0.796 vs win 0.545 = +0.250; market 0.512 vs 0.545 = -0.034; model Brier 0.319 vs market 0.247 side-space). Bootstrap on live 44 (10k resamples): gap point +0.250, 95% CI [+0.102,+0.400], frac<=0 = 0.0006 -> not a small-sample fluke. Independent 3502-bucket calib set at live width (sigma=5.0F, 2.78C, replicating _fitted_bucket_prob backend/data/weather.py:426-450): full-set reliability table shows model overconfident in 0.2-0.5 YES bins, market calibrated in every bin (MODEL Brier 0.069 vs MARKET 0.051 YES-space). Simulated bot bet-selection (8% edge gate + market-gap guardrail) on calib set: 939 bets, MODEL side-prob 0.452 vs realized 0.252 (+0.200), MARKET 0.250 vs 0.252 (-0.002) on identical buckets; NO bets 0.844 vs 0.545 (+0.299). Width sensitivity 4/5/6F: gap +0.190/+0.200/+0.210 (robust, not knife-edge). Per-city (all 6 active): chicago +0.179, denver +0.182, hong_kong +0.279, nyc +0.130, paris +0.173, tokyo +0.257 -> NOT one city. Excluding 06:00-UTC market-proxy leakage cities (tokyo+hong_kong): gap still +0.164. Sanity: model mean pred YES 0.0954 == realized base-rate 0.0954 (distribution integrates correctly), market 0.104 vs 0.101.
verdicts.caveats: The overconfidence is concentrated in the model's mid-confidence / traded region (YES prob 0.2-0.5, i.e. NO-side prob 0.5-0.85), not uniform across all buckets; far-tail high-confidence buckets are calibrated. The market-side calibration in the calib set could be marginally flattered by the 06:00-UTC proxy for Tokyo/HK, but the live-DB market calibration (0.512 vs 0.545) uses the real entry price with no proxy and confirms the market is calibrated, and the MODEL-vs-realized gap is measured against outcomes alone (market-independent), so leakage cannot manufacture it. Gap+fix framing: the deployed day-ahead width (max(raw*2.04,2.0)+0.7/lead ~= 5.0F, backend/config.py:157-159,203) is too narrow for the center error it sits on -> recalibrate by widening toward the realized dispersion / market-implied width, or shrink the model side-prob toward the market price (Platt/isotonic from this exact reliability curve) before the edge gate and Kelly sizing. Until then edge_at_entry is anti-predictive and the model loses to a calibrated market.

verdicts.claim: No honest slice (city/metric/lead) shows the model beating the market on Brier after costs with adequate sample; the apparent winners (HK n=6, London n=3) are small-sample variance.
verdicts.strongest_counter_evidence: CHICAGO is a genuine, OOS-robust slice where the model's FORECAST beats the market on Brier — refuting the broad reading "no honest slice anywhere," though the live bot throws the edge away via over-wide width. From calib_records.json (contested market in [0.05,0.95], n=211 buckets/54 events, event-clustered bootstrap): at a narrow honest width ~1.5F, chicago model_Brier=0.119 vs market 0.146, market-model=+0.026, 95% CI [+0.0047,+0.0464] EXCLUDES 0. Robust across bands ([0.1,0.9]: +0.037 CI[+0.010,+0.063]; [0.02,0.98]: +0.021 CI[+0.005,+0.037]) and across 3 out-of-sample time splits (train-best width on earlier dates applied to held-out later dates: +0.025/+0.033/+0.043, train-best width consistently 1.25F). Chicago beats the market for ALL widths <= 3.25F. Inland/EU block (chicago+nyc+denver+paris, no Asia leak) at 1.5F TIES the market: market-model=+0.011 CI[-0.0004,+0.0218]. The inland forecast CENTER has real skill; the live-width deficit is a width/calibration defect, not absence of edge.
verdicts.robustness_checks: (1) LIVE-WIDTH scoring (5.0F; 5.0/1.8=2.78C), the width the bot actually prices day-ahead — claim HOLDS hard: whole active set model_Brier=0.191 vs market 0.148, market-model=-0.043, event-bootstrap CI[-0.056,-0.030]. EVERY active city model-worse or tie-centered-negative: chicago -0.017 CI[-0.037,+0.002], nyc -0.016 CI[-0.037,+0.004], denver -0.023 CI[-0.045,+0.000], paris -0.036 CI[-0.057,-0.016], hong_kong -0.117 CI[-0.205,-0.038], tokyo -0.156 CI[-0.213,-0.098]. NO slice has CI>0 at the live width. (2) WIDTH SWEEP 1.5->6.0F on whole set: model only reaches parity at the in-sample-overfit 1.5F (-0.007 CI[-0.021,+0.005]); monotonically worse as width widens to the live 5.0F. Confirms the over-wide live sigma (inflation 2.04) is THE defect. (3) HK/Tokyo "winners" REFUTED as skill: at live width they are the WORST forecasting slices (HK calib 0.259 vs 0.142; tokyo 0.287 vs 0.132). Live DB (tradingbot_copy.db, 44 settled): HK +$626/83%-win sits on model_Brier 0.553 vs market 0.252 (n=6) — pure variance; active-total model 0.333 vs market 0.260. (4) ASIA 06:00-UTC LEAK quantified and direction confirmed: 06:00 UTC = 14-15h local (post-high); Asia high market is 80% at rails, Brier 0.033-0.041 vs 0.056 inland -> leak INFLATES MARKET skill, making the model's Asia deficit look WORSE than a fair day-ahead price; correcting it shrinks but cannot flip a -0.10..-0.16 gap. (5) The one survivor (chicago narrow-width) checked across 3 contested bands + 3 OOS time splits (above) — robust. (6) metric split at 5.0F: high -0.043 CI[-0.056,-0.030], low -0.049 CI[-0.142,+0.031] (low n=36 too small).
verdicts.caveats: GAP + FIX framing: The claim holds for the model AS DEPLOYED (live ~5.0F day-ahead width = max(raw*2.04,2.0)+0.7*lead): zero honest slice beats the market, and HK/London apparent P&L wins are variance + the Asia market-proxy leak. BUT the model's forecast CENTER is good inland — chicago's center beats the market's implied probabilities with CI>0 OOS, and inland/EU ties — so the deficit is a WIDTH/calibration over-correction, not missing skill. FIX: replace the single global sigma_inflation (2.04 -> ~5.0F) with per-city day-ahead width calibration; chicago's optimal is ~1.25-2F and it stays a winner up to 3.25F. Two important limits on the caveat: (a) "after costs" is NOT demonstrated — Brier excludes the 8% net-edge gate + spread, chicago's +0.026 Brier edge is modest, and NO strategy P&L backtest exists anywhere, so even chicago is unproven profitable after costs; (b) the fix does NOT rescue Asia — Tokyo/HK have genuinely bad centers (coastal, Tokyo un-bias-corrected) and lose at every width, so they should be cut, not re-widened. Net: the literal claim holds; the deeper claim "the model has no edge anywhere" is refuted by chicago/inland, which is the realistic path to profitability.
